description: This figure displays saturation magnetization curves (4πJs, in Gauss) versus carbon content (0.8–1.06 wt%) for Fe-30Mn-9Al-C austenitic steels subjected to various aging treatments. The graph contains multiple curves representing aging temperatures ranging from 350°C to 550°C with durations of 16 hours (dashed lines) and 100 hours (solid lines). The data reveals that saturation magnetization increases with carbon content across all conditions, with maximum values reaching approximately 2200–2400 G at 450–500°C for 100 hours, indicating extensive precipitation of magnetic κ-phase particles. In contrast, aging at lower temperatures (350–400°C) yields significantly lower magnetization values (<1000 G), suggesting limited κ-phase formation. The caption states that carbon is the most diffusion-mobile element and serves as the leading component at the onset of aging. These magnetic measurements provide qualitative information on the intensity of the austenite aging process and the kinetics of κ-phase precipitation in Fe-Mn-Al-C alloys.
